summary: Broad item-system matching and cleanup PR touching 38 files, primarily item TUs and headers, with small ground type/prototype updates. The bot report records +2920 matched-code bytes, 8 new matches, 45 unmatched-item improvements, and one .sdata2 regression in it_266F. Review feedback focused on replacing placeholder/permuter-style local names such as new_var with meaningful names.

key_files.role: major item matching/cleanup changes around item pick tables, random item spawning, local typing, loop cleanup, and matching tactics

what_changed: Changed 38 files: src/melee/gr/ground.c, src/melee/gr/ground.h, src/melee/gr/types.h, src/melee/it/forward.h, src/melee/it/itCharItems.h, src/melee/it/it_266F.c, src/melee/it/it_266F.dox, src/melee/it/it_266F.h, src/melee/it/it_2725.c, src/melee/it/it_2725.h, src/melee/it/itcoll.c, src/melee/it/items/it_2E5A.c, src/melee/it/items/itdosei.c, src/melee/it/items/itkyasarin.c, src/melee/it/items/itleadead.c, src/melee/it/items/itleadead.h, src/melee/it/items/itlikelike.c, src/melee/it/items/itlikelike.h, src/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.c, src/melee/it/items/itlinkboomerang.c, src/melee/it/items/itlinkhookshot.c, src/melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser.c, src/melee/it/items/itmewtwoshadowball.c, src/melee/it/items/itnessyoyo.c, src/melee/it/items/itoctarock.c, src/melee/it/items/itoctarock.h, src/melee/it/items/itoldkuri.c, src/melee/it/items/itoldkuri.h, src/melee/it/items/itoldottosea.c, src/melee/it/items/itpikachuthunder.c, src/melee/it/items/itsamusgrapple.c, src/melee/it/items/itsamusmissile.c, src/melee/it/items/itseakchain.c, src/melee/it/items/itseakneedlethrown.c, src/melee/it/items/itwhispyapple.c, src/melee/it/items/itwhitebea.c, src/melee/it/items/itwhitebea.h, src/melee/it/types.h.

Renamed or retyped unknown item allocation/table structs in forward declarations and declarations: HSD_ObjAllocUnk4 became RandomItemSpawner and HSD_ObjAllocUnk6 became ItemPickTable in visible diff excerpts.
Updated it_266F function signatures and call sites to use ItemPickTable* and RandomItemSpawner* instead of less-specific unknown allocation types.
Retyped StageInfo.xA0 from u8[4] to s32 and updated Ground_801C2AD8 to return s32* plus corresponding header and call-site changes.
Reworked item spawn/pick-table loops in it_8026CB9C, it_8026CF04, and it_8026D018 using named locals such as counts, mask, weight, spawner, tables, common, monster, cumulative, and idx.

Introduced some explicit matching tactics in visible excerpts, including #pragma dont_inline wrappers, stack padding arrays, volatile local cnt2, local declaration/order changes, and temporary ret/new_var locals.
Bot report recorded +0.08% matched code, +2920 bytes, 8 new matches, 45 improvements in unmatched items, and one unmatched-item .sdata2 regression for main/melee/it/it_266F.
smart_moves: Where supported by visible diff, unknown struct names were replaced by narrower domain names such as RandomItemSpawner and ItemPickTable, improving readability and reducing raw pointer-style access.
StageInfo.xA0 and Ground_801C2AD8 were updated together across source, header, and type definition, keeping the prototype aligned with the recovered field type.
Several generated-looking variable names in it_266F were replaced with role-oriented names such as counts, mask, weight, spawner, tables, common, monster, cumulative, and idx.
The PR body explicitly scoped remaining work pressure in item TUs, and the bot report quantified both matches and regressions, making progress and risk searchable.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered body proves that an unknown helper/data struct has a narrower domain role, update the forward declaration, global declarations, prototypes, and call sites together rather than leaving HSD_ObjAllocUnk-style names in the interface.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: forward.h HSD_ObjAllocUnk4/HSD_ObjAllocUnk6 renamed to RandomItemSpawner/ItemPickTable
raw_diff: it_266F.h prototypes changed to ItemPickTable*

decomp_lessons.lesson: If a field is recovered from a byte array to a scalar, update both address-taking call sites and getter return types so consumers no longer need casts or byte-array assumptions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ground.c Ground_801C0754 passes &stage_info.xA0 and Ground_801C2AD8 returns &stage_info.xA0

decomp_lessons.lesson: Reviewer-visible placeholder names such as new_var are treated as cleanup debt even if they help a match; prefer a minimal role name such as pos_copy when the role is known.
evidence_refs: review_comments: PsiLupan requested meaningful names instead of new_var and suggested pos_copy
review_comments: author replied that a lot of this is permuterslop and would get cleanup

naming_conventions.convention: Use domain names for recovered item table/spawner structs when evidence supports them, e.g. RandomItemSpawner and ItemPickTable rather than HSD_ObjAllocUnk4/HSD_ObjAllocUnk6.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_266F.c and it_2725.c type changes

naming_conventions.convention: Avoid placeholder locals like new_var in reviewable decomp code; even simple role names such as pos_copy are preferred when the role is clear.
Visible cleanup moved several generated arg/temp names toward role names such as counts, mask, weight, spawner, tables, common, monster, cumulative, and idx.

review_feedback.feedback: Requested meaningful names instead of placeholders like new_var; said this was the main complaint about most of the PR and suggested pos_copy as better than new_var.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.feedback: Acknowledged the issue, calling much of it permuterslop, and said they would give it a cleanup pass.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Avoid placeholder permuter locals in reviewable decomp PRs
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2460, review feedback called out placeholder names like new_var and requested meaningful names, with pos_copy given as an example of an acceptable simple role name. The author acknowledged that much of the PR was permuterslop and planned cleanup.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Propagate recovered struct roles through headers and declarations
accepted_candidate_records.text: The visible diff replaces HSD_ObjAllocUnk4/HSD_ObjAllocUnk6 with RandomItemSpawner/ItemPickTable in forward declarations, globals, and it_266F prototypes/call sites, illustrating that recovered domain types should be propagated across interface surfaces when supported.

source_update_candidates.title: Strengthen naming guidance against placeholder permuter locals
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding PR #2460 as historical evidence under conservative-naming or matching-tactics: reviewer feedback explicitly objected to placeholder names such as new_var in matched/permuter-shaped code and preferred even a simple role name like pos_copy.
evidence_refs: review_comments: PsiLupan requested meaningful names instead of new_var
review_comments: lukechampine called much of it permuterslop
source_update_candidates.reason: The existing conservative-naming standard already covers meaningful local names; this PR provides a concrete review example that placeholder permuter locals create cleanup debt.

source_update_candidates.title: Item random spawning code uses RandomItemSpawner and ItemPickTable names in it_266F/it_2725
source_update_candidates.text: Visible PR #2460 diff retyped item spawning globals/prototypes from HSD_ObjAllocUnk4/HSD_ObjAllocUnk6 to RandomItemSpawner/ItemPickTable across src/melee/it/forward.h, src/melee/it/it_266F.h, src/melee/it/it_266F.c, src/melee/it/it_266F.dox, and src/melee/it/it_2725.c.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future path-scoped lookup for item spawn table naming, but should be verified against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.text: Visible PR #2460 diff changes StageInfo.xA0 from u8[4] to s32 and Ground_801C2AD8 from u8* to s32*, including call-site address-taking in Ground_801C0754.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact for future stage/item interaction searches; should be checked against current source before adding.

rejection_notes: Do not promote #pragma dont_inline, volatile locals, _padA arrays, or ret/new_var temporaries as accepted tactics from this PR alone; the loaded evidence shows their use but not per-tactic verifier justification.
Do not infer that the .sdata2 regression was accepted as desirable; the bot report only records one regression.
Do not infer detailed semantics for every changed item file because the raw diff excerpt is truncated and many changed files only have metadata.
